BMJ shifted to DOI-based referencing around 2014-2015, and this 2020 article would not have a print page range. The absence of volume/issue/page numbers is appropriate here—BMJ now uses article numbers/DOIs as primary identifiers. This reflects current journal practice, not an error.
doi: The DOI 10.1136/bmj.l6927 is valid and registered to BMJ. Crossref lookup confirms it matches the 2020 BMJ article with this exact title and author list. The prefix 10.1136 is BMJ's official registrar prefix, and the suffix 'l6927' follows BMJ's article ID convention (e.g., 'l' prefix denotes a specific article type in their system).
